courses.title: Dance Appreciation
courses.description: When does moving become dancing? When does a dance party become a community celebration? How does dancing highlight important cultural, historical, or personal moments? Dance Appreciation is designed to help us see how people dance and how dancing shapes the way communities and societies see themselves. This course is an introduction to dance studies and dance history, a survey of a variety of dance forms, styles, and movements, including hip hop, hula, Folklórico de México, ballet, jazz dance, contemporary dance, social dance, and other diasporic traditions. Dance Appreciation introduces vocabularies for describing physical and social movements through viewing and discussing a broad range of dance performances and events. By actively looking at dances, students will deepen their ability to see and describe art and strengthen their analytical skills and ability to articulate their critical perspective. May be offered in a distance-learning format. CAB GE C, Cal-GETC 3A.

courses.description: Prepares students for participation in cybersecurity competitions. Covers major ethical and legal issues concerning online safety, open-source intelligence, and cryptography. courses.title: Enterprise Networking, Security, and Automation
courses.description: Covers the architecture, components, operations, and security to scale for large, complex networks, including wide area network (WAN) technologies and quality of service (QoS) concepts. Emphasizes network security concepts and introduces network virtualization and automation. Covers concepts and configuration of OSPFv2, IPv4 ACLs, NAT, DHCP, and SLAAC on enterprise network devices. courses.title: Income Tax I, Individual
courses.description: Want to gain the skills to confidently prepare taxes and launch a career in one of the most in-demand fields? This course covers fundamental principles of federal and California individual income taxation, including tax planning, compliance, and preparation. Real-world applications are also emphasized through computerized tax projects and hands-on practice with current tax software. Students receive the preparation necessary to meet the California Tax Education Council (CTEC) Qualifying Education requirements for tax preparers, making this course an ideal choice either for those pursuing a career in tax or seeking practical knowledge for personal or professional use. May be offered in a distance-learning format. Recommended preparation: ACCT-151A (Introduction toýAccounting: Bookkeeping Concepts) or ACCT-1A (FinancialýAccounting)

courses.title: Survey of Art From Prehistory to the Medieval Era
courses.description: This course introduces students to visual art and architecture from prehistory to the medieval era with a focus on art from Europe, North Africa, and the near East. The course will further consider global interactions involving these regions. Through in-class discussion and visual and contextual analysis, students will develop visual and historical literacy by studying objects, images, and structures. Students will expand their worldview by recognizing the contributions of historic peoples to the development of human cultures and how they continue to impact society today. May be offered in a distance-learning format. CAB GE C, Cal-GETC 3A. C-ID: ARTH 110.

courses.title: Latinx Images in Photo-Based Media
courses.description: Examines the role of Latinx photography and lens-based media in shaping perspectives on race and ethnicity, class, gender, sexuality, immigration status, and national identity in the United States. May be offered in a distance-learning format. CAB GE C, Cal-GETC 3A.

courses.description: This course is grounded in the theoretical foundations and practice of public speaking and small-group communication in a diverse democratic society. Through the study of rhetorical theory, students learn to successfully communicate ideas of an informative and persuasive nature while applying effective small-group theories, concepts, and techniques, such as problem-solving, conflict management, decision-making, and leadership. Students work individually and collaboratively in teams to successfully prepare and present oral presentations delivered in the public speaking mode (one-to-many) that must be faculty-supervised, faculty-evaluated, and delivered to a live audience. Emphasis is placed on critical thinking and listening, where students apply rhetorical principles to evaluate the speeches of others and analyze historical and contemporary discourse. May be offered in a distance-learning format. Transfers to CSU; UC, with limits: COMM 2 & 10 combined-maximum credit-1 course. CAB GE A2. C-ID: COMM 140.

courses.title: Argumentation and Persuasion
courses.description: Discusses and applies oral and written argumentation concepts to develop critical thinking skills. Oral argumentation focuses on the principles of logical reasoning and then applies these principles to persuasive speeches and policy debates. Written argumentation focuses on the principles of rhetorical analysis and then applies these principles to speeches, debates, and essays. Students utilize and critically analyze research and documentation while constructing and evaluating oral and written arguments. A minimum of 5,000 words of writing is required. May be offered in a distance-learning format. CAB GE A2. C-ID: COMM 120. Prerequisite: ENGL-C1000 (Academic Reading andýWriting) orýENGL-C1000H (Academic Reading and Writing - Honors) orýENGL-1AMC (CollegeýComposition: Multicultural Emphasis) or ENGL-1AMCH (HonorsýCollege Composition: Multicultural Emphasis) or ESL-1Aý(College Composition for ESL)ýRecommended preparation: COMM-C1000 (Introduction to PublicýSpeaking) or COMM-C1000Hý(Introduction to Public Speaking - Honors)

courses.title: Administration II: Personnel and Leadership in Early Childhood Education
courses.description: Emphasizes effective strategies for personnel management and the leadership role of the director in early care and education settings. Topics include legal and ethical responsibilities, supervision techniques, professional development, goal-setting, team-building, assessment, record keeping, and reflective practices for a diverse and inclusive early care and education program. Meets the requirements for three units in Program Administration for Social Services Licensing and three of the required six units for the Site Supervisor level of the California Child Development Permit. May be offered in a distance-learning format. Prerequisite: ECE-31 orýECE-131 (Child Growth and Development)ýRecommended preparation: ECE-51ý(Principles of Working with Young Children), ECE-53ý(Introduction to Curriculum for the Young Child), and ECE-32ýOR ECE-142ý(Child, Family and Community)

courses.title: Human Sexuality
courses.instructor: Alyssa S. Steiner, Gina M. Lepore
courses.description: Examines human sexuality topics and contemporary issues with a socio-psychological, biological, multicultural, and historical focus. Emphasizes sex positivity and inclusiveness in discussions of gender, sexual orientation, diverse sexual behaviors, and sexual health. Additional topics include sex research, structure and function of the sexual and reproductive systems, physiology of human sexual response, sexual violence prevention, communication, and healthy relationships. May be offered in a distance-learning format. CAB GE D, E, Cal-GETC 4. C-ID: PSY 130.

courses.title: Physical Geography
courses.instructor: Deirdre C. Scholar, Keith A. Ducote
courses.description: Studies Earth's dynamic physical systems and processes, including Earth-sun geometry, weather, climate, water, landforms, soil, and the biosphere. Analyzes maps and mapping techniques (GIS) as a geographic tool used to solve geographic problems. Emphasizes interrelationships between human and environmental systems. Includes investigation and analysis of inequities in geographic processes such as the disproportionate impacts of climate change on minoritized communities and solutions for mitigating these inequities through environmental justice practices. May be offered in a distance-learning format.

courses.title: Elementary American Sign Language II
courses.description: Continues the study of American Sign Language (ASL), practicing and building upon foundational ASL skills. Develops receptive skills for language comprehension and expressive signing skills for language production. Examines features of Deaf culture that influence the use of the language in daily life. Mechanics of the language will be firmly couched in the tenets of Deaf culture. Incorporates a particular focus on the impact of race, gender, religion, sexual orientation, and ethnicity, on the individual's Deaf cultural identity. May be offered in a distance-learning format. CAB GE C. Prerequisite: ASL-1 (Elementary American Sign Language I)
